profile.name: David
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to handle the household’s investing and paperwork, especially retirement accounts and anything with statements/tax forms.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable discussing account types (taxable vs. IRA/401k) and tradeoffs, but may want the advisor to translate tax strategy into plain steps.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk in practice: willing to stay invested and tolerate normal market swings, but becomes cautious when decisions feel irreversible (e.g., locking money up, complex products).
profile.communication_style: Measured and detail-oriented; will pause to think, uses qualifiers like “I believe” and “if I’m remembering right,” and may correct small number mix-ups. Asks for definitions when jargon shows up, prefers a clear recap at the end.
emotional_tendencies: anxious about making a mistake late in life
relieved by clear structure and checklists
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and sequential: wants to review statements, understand pros/cons, then decide; prefers to sleep on bigger moves and come back with questions.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: open to guidance but wants transparency on fees, taxes, and why a recommendation fits their situation.
internal_biases: loss aversion (dislikes seeing account values drop, even if temporary)
status quo bias (prefers incremental changes over big reallocations)
hidden_concerns: worries about healthcare or longevity costs outlasting income
concerned about taxes being higher than expected and making the wrong withdrawal choices

profile.name: Linda
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; more focused on day-to-day cash flow and lifestyle, and tends to speak up about what feels comfortable month-to-month.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands the basics of income coming in and bills going out, but mixes up terms (gross vs. net, premium vs. benefit, balance vs. payment) and prefers round numbers.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate-to-slightly conservative: likes stability and predictability; may push for keeping more in cash when markets feel uncertain, even if she agrees with a long-term plan in calmer periods.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and quick; uses phrases like “roughly,” “give or take,” and “I’m not sure exactly,” may interrupt to clarify a practical point. Responds well to simple examples and “what does this mean for us each month?” framing.
emotional_tendencies: reassured by steady routines and clear next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Values-based and collaborative: decides based on comfort level and lifestyle impact; often looks to David for the final call but will veto anything that feels too risky or complicated.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Warm but verification-seeking: tends to like the advisor personally, yet wants to hear the same point explained two ways before feeling confident.
internal_biases: mental accounting (separates ‘safe money’ vs. ‘investment money’ in her head)
recency bias (recent market headlines can sway her comfort level)
hidden_concerns: worries about maintaining independence and not becoming a burden
concerned about having enough readily available cash for surprises without ‘touching investments’
